violations.text: Molluscan shellfish tags must be maintained for 90 days in chronological order and the date when the last shellfish was used from the bag must be noted on the shellfish tag. PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Various foods were observed held at 46-48 degrees Fahrenheit. Foods were assessed, TCS foods that were out of temperature for an unknown amount of time were voluntarily disposed of. Foods recently purchased were placed into other refrigeration. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

Crab meat was observed thawing in stagnant water. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/05/2025

2 door reach-in was observed at ranging ambient temperatures of 46-50 degrees Fahrenheit. Unit was taken out of service to hold foods that require refrigeration for food safety. Equipment to be in good repair and proper adjustment. New Violation. Correct By: 06/19/2025

Chicken observed at 80 degrees F. and cooling in the 2 door True refrigerator. Per interview of staff, chicken was prepared 1 hour prior to this inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed. -Staff moved the chicken to the freezer to allow for rapid cooling during this inspection. -Establishment shall keep a cooling food temperature log for foods in the cooling process. -Cooling food temperature log shall be completed and provided to Inspector upon the next inspection date. *The 2 door True refrigerator may not be adequate for cooling process. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/29/2023

Per interview of staff, the 3 bay sink is used as a food prep sink. 3 bay sink shall be air gapped with do direct connection. -An air gap between the water supply inlet and the flood level rim of the PLUMBING FIXTURE, EQUIPMENT, or nonFOOD EQUIPMENT shall be at least twice the diameter of the water supply inlet and may not be less than 25 mm (1 inch). New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023
